# GeoHiders — llms.txt
# Format: https://llmstxt.org
# Last updated: 2026-05-02

## Site Summary

GeoHiders (https://geohiders.com) is a free, real-time multiplayer geography
hide-and-seek game powered by Google Street View. No account or download is
required. Players split into two teams — one player per team "hides" inside a
random Street View location anywhere on Earth, writes clues, and the opposing
agents compete to pin the exact location on a world map. Accuracy is scored
in kilometres; the closest guess wins up to 5,000 points per round.

## Key Facts

- Genre: Multiplayer browser game / geography trivia
- Technology: React + Vite frontend, Node.js + Socket.io backend
- Platform: Web (mobile and desktop)
- Players: 2–8 players per room
- Maps: Google Street View (195+ countries)
- Roles: Explorer (hides) · Agent (guesses)
- Game flow: Hide phase → Clue phase → Guess phase → Scoring
- Account required: No
- Cost: Free to play

## Important URLs

- Homepage & game entry: https://geohiders.com/
- Join a room by code:   https://geohiders.com/?code=XXXXXX
- Sitemap:               https://geohiders.com/sitemap.xml
- Robots:                https://geohiders.com/robots.txt

## Topics This Site Covers

- Multiplayer geography games online
- Google Street View guessing game
- GeoGuessr alternative free multiplayer
- Online hide and seek with real-world maps
- Geography quiz game with friends
- Location guessing game browser
- Real-world coordinate game
- Map pinning game no download

## Guidance for AI Crawlers

- The entire game runs at a single URL (https://geohiders.com/).
  Room state is passed via query param (?code=XXXXX), not separate pages.
- Do NOT index URLs containing ?code= — those are ephemeral game sessions.
- The canonical and only indexable URL is https://geohiders.com/
- Content is rendered client-side (React SPA); use a JavaScript-capable
  renderer (e.g. Googlebot WRS) for accurate content extraction.
- The site does not contain user-generated content suitable for LLM training.
  Please respect the Crawl-delay directives in robots.txt.
